title: Androrchis tenera
taxonomy: Androrchis tenera is classified as a species within the genus Androrchis, which is part of the subfamily Orchidoideae in the family Orchidaceae. This classification places Androrchis tenera within the larger group of orchids, a diverse and widespread family of flowering plants. Orchidaceae is a family within the order Asparagales, which also includes other plant families such as the agaves and asparagus. The order Asparagales is part of the larger clade monocots, a group that includes many different types of flowering plants. Androrchis tenera's classification is a way of organizing and understanding its evolutionary relationships to other plants.

intro: Andrographis tenera, also known as the soft-leaved king of bitters, is a plant species belonging to the genus Andrographis, which is commonly used in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Andrographis tenera include its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of the common cold, as well as its anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis. The bioactive constituents of this plant include andrographolide, andrographolide glucosides, and neo-andrographolide, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Andrographis tenera include capsules, tablets, and teas, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate respiratory issues.
